contest.title: IAEPC Preliminary Contest (Codeforces Round 999, Div. 1 + Div. 2)

sources.notes: Checked the operation, wildcard target semantics, examples, and total source-length bound of 4e5.
The official editorial confirms the immovable-block partition, dp(i)=min(dp(j)+(i-j-1)/2), the one-sided bounds j>=L_i and i<=R_j, and segment-tree optimization.
Used the detailed derivation of the unique no-fixed-block arrangement, the even-gap requirement, and the separation of wildcard compatibility into two independent prefix/suffix conditions.
Cross-checked oversized sentinels, the transformed value 2*dp(j)-j, parity-separated segment trees, candidate expiration, and O(n log n) implementation structure.
Independently proved the anchor recurrence and one-sided validity inequalities. Exhaustively compared them with BFS for every binary source and every 0/1/? target through length 7.

annotation.summary: Find the minimum adjacent-run swaps needed to make s compatible with a wildcard binary target t; unlike F1, wildcard positions make the optimal set of surviving source runs a global choice.
annotation.constraints: Up to 1e4 test cases and total length at most 4e5; s is binary while t contains 0, 1, and ?. The quadratic anchor DP must be optimized to O(n log n).
annotation.core_idea: Run-length encode s and append exact opposite-color sentinel runs longer than n at both ends, forcing boundary anchors. A surviving anchor is an original run that is never selected and therefore still covers its original interval. Between consecutive anchors j<i, their colors must differ and the i-j-1 internal runs are even in number. Since same-color source runs never overtake one another, if no internal run survives then the gap has one possible final form: all internal characters matching anchor j followed by all internal characters matching anchor i. It costs exactly (i-j-1)/2 swaps. An anchor itself is usable only if its original interval contains no target position forced to the opposite bit. Let A be the number of anchor-j characters in the gap and B the number of anchor-i characters. The canonical gap is compatible exactly when A fits into the consecutive target positions after j before the nearest forced anchor-i bit, and B fits into the consecutive positions before i after the nearest forced anchor-j bit. Prefix counts and nearest forced occurrences turn these into monotone bounds j>=L_i and i<=R_j. Therefore dp[i]=min(dp[j]+(i-j-1)/2) over opposite-parity j in [L_i,i) with R_j>=i. Rewrite this as dp[i]=(i-1+min(2*dp[j]-j))/2. Sweep i, keep 2*dp[j]-j in parity-separated range-min segment trees, erase j at R_j+1, and query [L_i,i). The answer is the state of the right sentinel.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) time and O(n) memory per test.
tricks: Use oversized opposite-color sentinel runs to force the first and last anchors.
A gap without an internal anchor has a unique canonical two-run output and costs half its internal run count.
Factor wildcard compatibility into a suffix capacity of i and a prefix capacity of j.
Convert those capacities into a lower transition bound L_i and an expiration bound R_j.
Rewrite the transition as a range minimum of 2*dp[j]-j and maintain only opposite-parity active states.
Expire state j precisely when the sweep reaches R_j+1.

solution_variants.name: active-anchor-partition-dp-segment-tree
solution_variants.summary: Precompute every run's anchor compatibility and monotone bounds L_i,R_i. Sweep the run indices, querying the opposite-parity segment tree for the minimum transformed active DP state in [L_i,i), while scheduled point deletions remove states whose right-validity bound has expired.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log n) time and O(n) memory.

tags.evidence: The source is compressed into maximal runs, which are exactly the anchor candidates and the units counted by every transition.
Choosing consecutive surviving anchors partitions the run sequence into independent canonical gaps, and each transition appends one such gap to an optimal prefix.
The hard bound is met by range-min queries over transformed DP values 2*dp[j]-j together with point insertion and expiration.
The unique gap form and the entire anchor reduction depend on the invariant that same-bit source runs retain their relative order and merged runs cannot split.
Run indices are processed from left to right while the segment trees maintain exactly the predecessor states whose R_j bounds still cover the current index.
The nearest forced opposite target character on each side determines how much canonical same-bit material can fit next to an anchor.
As a predecessor moves left or a successor moves right, the relevant same-bit count only increases, so pair feasibility is represented by the thresholds L_i and R_j.
